rationale: Read the real tree at 001ed3a5. (a) GateFacts are built in resolvePanelGateFacts (panel.go:302-315) via ResolveGateFacts; the GateIO.RevParse closure is caller-supplied, so a non-bead override that ignores gate.go's 'bead/'+beadID ref (gate.go:372) and rev-parses reg.Panel.Target feeds live-vs-reviewed SHA into the SAME legs — un-shadowing (6)/(8)-(10). Zero internal/panel edit. (b) TRUE: Decision.Message is a returned string field on a value type; the CLI owns it post-call. Leg SELECTION is fixed purely by the facts the closure gathers (which leg fires is a function of MissingRef/GitErr/HeadSHA), NOT by editing PanelGateDecision. String cosmetics (RawMergeFence(''), 'references branch bead/,') are stripped by a CLI sanitize. `git show --name-only` listing none of internal/panel|instruct|complete is genuinely achievable. (c) sanitizeNonBeadDecision takes/returns Decision by value, gated on non-bead, never touches Action; bead pins bypass it and survive byte-identical. (d) Fence holds: complete's panelGate short-circuits on beadID=='' and matches only ForBead panels (panel_advisory.go:141-152) — never non-bead; instruct's verdict() sets HeadSHA/MissingRef only when IsBead() (panelstate.go:108), leaving legs (5)/(5b)/(6) inert for non-bead, so it never emits the malformed leg-5 path. Design is sound.
findings: GROUNDED: leg-5 constant is 'panel for %s references branch bead/%s,' (gate.go:188); with beadID='' the sanitize match 'references branch bead/,' is exact. leg-5b 'could not verify branch bead/%s' (gate.go:201) and RawMergeFence('') = the git-merge-bead/ empty fence (gate.go:58-63) are all real; the plan's four rewrite rules target exactly these.

GROUNDED: executor seam revParseForPanelFn (panel.go:37-39) + exec.RevParseRef/IsRefNotFound wired at panel.go:310-312; panelBeadWorktreePath returns '' for beadID=='' (panel.go:322-325) BEFORE panelWorktreeListFn, so the one-rev-parse / no-worktree-list ADR-0030 budget claim is correct.

GROUNDED: the closure ignoring the ref arg is legitimate — ResolveGateFacts also routes IsRefNotFound classification through the same caller-supplied seam, so genuinely-deleted target -> MissingRef -> leg(5) Warn, transient -> GitErr -> leg(5b) Warn, advanced -> leg(6) Block. Un-advanced target reaches legs (8)-(10). All by facts alone.

NON-BLOCKING NOTE: instruct's SessionStart panel-state block STILL renders the cosmetic empty 'git merge bead/ ' fence for a non-bead below-threshold panel (verdict() consumes d.Message; legs 8-10 append RawMergeFence('')). R1 deliberately scopes this OUT to preserve the zero-diff, and the AC-global fence mandates instruct pass unmodified — so this is spec-sanctioned, and the plan explicitly documents it (plan lines 103-110). A future followup could clean it, but it does not affect R1's correctness.

VERIFIED: renderPanelTally returns d and panelTallyCmd threads it to tallyExitAction (panel.go:204-206); the plan sanitizes inside the renderers so the sanitized message flows to the exit/recovery path. tallyExitAction's hardcoded recovery (panel.go:532-533) is CLI-layer (in scope) and step 4's non-bead branch removing 'then mindspec complete <bead>' is coherent.

The zero-byte-diff-to-internal/panel claim (b) is the load-bearing assertion and it is TRUE: the bug's leg selection is fact-driven, not message-driven, so it is fixable entirely from the caller side.
